content.full_text:
HUMBOLDT-DEL NORTE COUNTY
DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH

727 CEDAR STREET                    MAIN OFFICE                      909 HWY. 101, NORTH
GARBERVILLE, CA. 95440              529 I STREET                     CRESCENT CITY, CA. 95531
923-2779                            EUREKA, CA. 95501               464-7227
                                    445-6200


January 10, 1991


Fair Political Practice Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95801

RE: Request For Written Opinion on the Applicability of the
Political Reform Act of 1974 to the Integrated Waste Management
Plan Local Task Force

Dear Sir:

Public Resources Code Section 40950 requires each county to
convene a Local Task Force to assist in the implementation of the
Integrated Waste Management Act of 1989.

As a result of this mandate the Humboldt County Board of
Supervisors passed Resolution 90-85 (copy enclosed). Within the
resolution under the heading of "membership" the various types of
representation are presented (ie recycling, solid waste operation,
solid waste collector, environmental groups, composting operation).

The task force membership has expressed concern about potential
conflicts of interest in that as members of the task force they may
be voting on issues that directly affect their business or in other
cases they may be refusing plans that their employer has prepared
for a city or the county.

The Humboldt County Counsel's Office provided an opinion that task
force members could not participate in discussions or decisions
concerning issues in which they have an economic interest. A copy
of that opinion is attached for your review. This limitation seems
to be in direct conflict with the purpose of the task force as defined
in the Integrated Waste Management Act. The purpose of the local task

task force, as we understand it, is to provide information from a
variety of affected interests in the planning process necessary to
develop an effective and coordinated integrated waste management
plan.

We would appreciate receiving guidance from you concerning any
methods that can be used by us to receive input from members of
the local task force to assure that informed input is received from
the parties affected by the development of an integrated waste
management plan.

Specifically, we are requesting a written opinion regarding the
applicability of the Political Reform Act to members of the Local
Task Force. Must the members file "Statements of Economic
Interest"?

Should there be any questions in this matter, please contact me at
(707) 445-6215.

Thank you.

Sincerely,


Lan Sing Wu, M.D., M.P.H.
Health Officer

[signature]

William Strickland, M.P.H.
Senior Environmental Health Specialist

WSkm

Enclosures

cc: AB939

sections.question: Specifically, we are requesting a written opinion regarding the applicability of the Political Reform Act to members of the Local Task Force. Must the members file "Statements of Economic Interest"?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether members of the Humboldt County Local Task Force for Integrated Waste Management must comply with the Political Reform Act, including whether they must file Statements of Economic Interest, given that task force membership includes representatives from industries that may have economic interests affected by task force decisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or formal Q/A section headers present. The specific question appears in the final paragraph. Document is a formal letter from county health officials seeking FPPC opinion on Political Reform Act applicability.

embedding.summary: Humboldt County Department of Public Health requests FPPC written guidance on whether the Political Reform Act applies to members of a Local Task Force established under the Integrated Waste Management Act, specifically whether task force members must file Statements of Economic Interest and how to balance conflict of interest restrictions with the task force's purpose of receiving input from affected industries.
